description: Create a GitLab CI pipeline with stages test, paperwork, and deploy and set OMP_NUM_THREADS='1' and PIP_CACHE_DIR to $CI_PROJECT_DIR/pip-cache. In the test stage add a bleeding-edge job using the python:3-alpine image, caching $PIP_CACHE_DIR under key bleeding-edge, running before_script pip install --upgrade numpy scipy matplotlib and pip install --editable .[test] then pip freeze, and script ase test --pytest --ignore gui/ --ignore test_imports.py. Also in test add a calculators job on registry.gitlab.com/ase/ase:ase-full-monty, caching $PIP_CACHE_DIR with key calculators (policy pull), before_script pip install --no-deps --editable .; export ASE_CONFIG_PATH=/home/ase/aseconfig.ini; sed 's/binary = /command = /' -i /home/ase/aseconfig.ini; ase info --calculators, script ase test calculator --calculators abinit,asap,cp2k,dftb,espresso,gpaw,kim,lammpslib,lammpsrun,mopac,nwchem,octopus,siesta --coverage --pytest --junit-xml=report.xml, rules allowing manual on push and automatic on schedule with allow_failure, after_script generating coverage xml and moving reports, and artifacts (coverage_report in cobertura format, junit report.xml) expiring in 1 week. Add a calculators-lite job with the same image and cache, before_script identical, script ase test calculator --calculators=auto --coverage --pytest -m calculator_lite --junit-xml=report.xml, and matching after_script and artifacts. In the paperwork stage add a coverage-combine job using registry.gitlab.com/ase/ase:ase-paperwork, depending on main and calculators-lite, script cd ase/test; coverage combine ../../coverage-main/coverage.dat ../../coverage-calculators-lite/coverage.dat; coverage report; coverage html; cd ../..; mv ase/test/coverage-html ., artifacts path coverage-html expiring in 1 week. Also in test add a distribution-package job on registry.gitlab.com/ase/ase:ase-main with before_script mkdir dist and pip install setuptools build, rules on schedule and manual on push (allow_failure), script python -m build; ls dist/*; pip install dist/ase-*.tar.gz; ase test; pip uninstall --yes ase; pip install dist/ase-*-py3-none-any.whl; ase test, artifacts dist expiring in 1 week. Add a doc job on ase-full-monty image, before_script pip install scriv sphinx_book_theme sphinx-gallery and pip install --no-deps .[docs]; ase info; which sphinx-build, cache key doc, script scriv collect --keep || echo Not compiling changelog then; python -m ase.utils.sphinx run; sphinx-build -W doc doc/build; mv doc/build web-html, artifacts web-html expiring in 1 week. Add a lint job on python:3.12.4-alpine, before_script pip install .[lint] and pip freeze, cache key lint, script mypy --version; mypy --color-output -p ase; python -We:invalid -m compileall -f -q ase/; ruff check; ruff format --check, variables PIP_CACHE_DIR as defined. Add a main job on ase-main image, before_script python --version; pip install --no-deps --editable .; ase info --calculators; cd $CI_PROJECT_DIR, cache key main, script ase test --calculators asap,ff,lj,morse,tip3p,tip4p --coverage --pytest --junit-xml=report.xml, after_script generating coverage xml and moving reports, artifacts coverage-main with coverage_report and junit, expiring in 1 week. Add an oldlibs job on ase-oldlibs image, before_script pwd; whoami; pip install specific old versions of numpy, scipy, matplotlib, pytest, etc.; pip install --no-deps ., cache key oldlibs, script ase test. In the deploy stage add a pages job that runs only on master, depends on coverage-combine and doc, script mkdir public; mv coverage-html public/; pwd; ls; mv web-html/* public/, artifacts public expiring in 2 weeks, publishing to GitLab Pages. Also add a manual pipinstall job on python:3.11 with before_script python --version; pip install .[test]; script ase test. Finally add a manual windows_test job on a Windows runner (tag saas-windows-medium-amd64) with before_script importing Chocolatey, installing python 3.10, refreshing env, installing pip, pytest, the package, and script ase test --calculators eam,ff,lj,morse,tip3p,tip4p --pytest with warning suppressions.
